prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and social change; underdiagnosis common

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with screening tools (GDS/PHQ) where used; differentiate from dementia and medical causes

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to limited psychotherapy and specialist access

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antidepressants and counseling where available; address social isolation; manage comorbidities; suicide risk assessment
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High stigma and limited geriatric mental health services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic antidepressants and expanding tele-mental health capacity
